ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / WYOMING

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Wyoming Dental Assistant Permit

Wyoming Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in Wyoming run through Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant through the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

  1. STEP 1

    Review Eligibility Requirements

    Confirm you meet the eligibility criteria for an Expanded Functions Dental Assistant (EFDA) registration. This includes possessing an active CPR certification, completing a board-approved EFDA training program (minimum 60 hours with a clinical component), and passing an EFDA written and clinical examination administered by the Western Regional Examining Board (WREB) or the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB).

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Download Application Form

    Download and print the 'Expanded Functions Dental Assistant (EFDA) Application' form directly from the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners EFDA Application page. Complete the form accurately and entirely.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Gather Supporting Documents

    Collect all required supporting documents as specified in the application packet. These typically include a copy of your active CPR certification, an original or certified copy of your EFDA training program certificate, official proof of passing your EFDA examination (WREB or DANB), and a photocopy of your current photo identification.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Prepare Application Fee

    Prepare a payment of $25.00 for the application fee. Ensure the payment method complies with the instructions on the application form (e.g., check or money order made payable to the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners).

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Mail Complete Application

    Assemble the completed application form, all supporting documents, and the application fee, and mail the entire package to the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners at the address provided on the application form.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Monitor Application Status

    After submission, allow time for the Board to process your application. You can use the Board's online License Lookup tool to verify your registration status once it has been issued.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$50
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$50

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in Wyoming follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$25
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

Does Wyoming require a dental assistant license?

Yes, Wyoming requires Dental Assistant through the state board.

Is DANB certification recognized in Wyoming?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Wyoming.

Can I take dental x-rays in Wyoming?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Wyoming?

State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

Not required
